events.desc:
No, not uniformly.

Pearl Harbor (Dec 1941): No recession—full expansion since 1938 trough; GDP rising on pre-war defense spending, exiting Depression.

Gulf War/Desert Storm (Jan 1991): Yes—recession July 1990–Mar 1991 already underway (oil shock + tight policy).

Afghanistan (Oct 2001): Yes—dot-com recession Mar–Nov 2001 ongoing (post-9/11 deepened it briefly).

Iraq (Mar 2003): No—recovery from 2001 recession; real GDP +2.8% in 2002, expansion through 2007.

Patterns vary; markets often dipped short-term on uncertainty then rebounded on spending/mobilization.
